report_name: Silver Fox APT Targets India and Russia with New 'ABCDoor' Backdoor

threat_actors.name: Silver Fox
threat_actors.description: A China-affiliated APT group conducting cyber-espionage campaigns against organizations in India and Russia, targeting industrial, consulting, retail, and transportation sectors.
threat_actors.goals: Intelligence gathering for economic or state advantage, including theft of intellectual property, trade secrets, or sensitive business information.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: The Silver Fox APT used malicious RAR archives in phishing emails targeting organizations in India.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The Silver Fox APT used links within PDF attachments that downloaded malicious ZIP archives in a campaign targeting Russian organizations, a technique intended to bypass email gateways.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: The infection chain relies on the user opening a malicious executable disguised with a PDF icon from a RAR or ZIP archive.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The RustSL loader downloads the ValleyRAT payload, which in turn is used to deliver the ABCDoor backdoor to the compromised system.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Python
attack_patterns.description: The ABCDoor backdoor is a Python-based malware, indicating the use of the Python interpreter for its execution on the victim's machine.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used a modified version of the open-source RustSL loader to act as an evasive first stage for their malware deployment.

malware.name: ABCDoor
malware.description: A new, previously undocumented Python-based backdoor deployed by the Silver Fox APT group for data exfiltration and remote control.

malware.name: ValleyRAT
malware.description: A known, modular remote access trojan used by Silver Fox as a primary payload to deliver the final ABCDoor backdoor.

malware.name: RustSL
malware.description: A modified version of an open-source shellcode loader written in Rust, used as the initial stage in the infection chain to download and execute ValleyRAT.

indicators.name: Suspicious Python Execution by ABCDoor Backdoor
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Python Execution from User Directory
id: 5b9d3c1a-8f7e-4b2d-9c8a-1e3f5d7a9b0c
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the execution of the Python interpreter from a user's download or temporary directory. This may indicate the execution of a malicious Python-based backdoor like ABCDoor, which is delivered via phishing.
references:
  - https://securelist.com/silver-fox-tax-campaign-abcdoor/112613/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-06
tags:
  - attack.t1059.006
  - attack.execution
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\python.exe'
    CurrentDirectory|contains:
      - 'C:\Users\'
  filter_legit_python_dev:
    CurrentDirectory|contains:
      - '\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\'
      - '\source\repos\'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_python_dev
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate execution of Python scripts by developers or data scientists from user directories.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects the execution of Python scripts from unusual locations, such as user download folders, which could indicate the presence of the Python-based ABCDoor backdoor.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Python Execution by ABCDoor Backdoor
